name: Belarus (1918, 1991-1995)

colors.symbolism: freedom, purity, and the historic 'White Ruthenia'
the blood shed in the struggle for Belarusian nationhood and the colour of the historic Pahonia

description: Three equal horizontal stripes — white, red, white — covering the field.
history: First adopted on 25 March 1918 as the flag of the short-lived Belarusian People's Republic, designed by Klaudziusz Duz-Dushewski. The republic fell within months and the flag was suppressed under Soviet rule, though it remained in use among diaspora Belarusians and pro-independence movements throughout the 20th century. It was readopted as the state flag of the Republic of Belarus on 19 September 1991 following the dissolution of the Soviet Union, and remained in official use until 7 June 1995, when President Alexander Lukashenko replaced it (after a controversial referendum) with a modified version of the Belarusian SSR flag. The white-red-white flag continues in widespread unofficial use as the symbol of the Belarusian democratic opposition.

features.symbolism: the historic Belarusian national colours, drawn from the Pahonia coat of arms and from medieval heraldry of the lands of the Grand Duchy of Lithuania
